description: Canonical content form (§4.3): fine-grained transform behaviours (marks normalization, text-node merge, derived-field and crdt stripping) and inputs a conformant canonicalizer MUST reject (§4.3.2).
oracle: Expected canonical objects are HAND-AUTHORED by reasoning about the §4.3 rules; their JCS bytes and digests come from the independent Python oracle at conformance/oracles/canonicalize_oracle.py (stdlib json + hashlib, sharing no code with this repository’s canonicalizer). A reject vector asserts only that a conformant implementation refuses the input; §4.3.2 makes these inputs ones a reader MUST reject, and canonicalization defects carry no portable code, so no identifier is asserted.

vectors.name: marks-empty-omitted
vectors.description: An empty marks array on a text node is omitted entirely (absent ≡ []); §4.3.1 item 3.

vectors.name: text-merge-differing-marks-preserved
vectors.description: Adjacent text nodes with equal (here empty) mark-sets merge; a node with a differing mark-set does not (§4.3.1 item 4).

vectors.name: crdt-stripped-from-block
vectors.description: A block’s crdt field is stripped and its author-chosen id is alpha-renamed to b0 (§4.3.1 items 1 and 5; §4.1a).

vectors.name: reject-duplicate-key-manifest
vectors.description: A duplicate key anywhere in a part (here manifest) is rejected by strict parsing (§4.3.2 item 3).

vectors.name: reject-non-nfc-text
vectors.description: A stored string not in Unicode NFC is rejected, never silently normalized (§4.3.2). Here “cafe” + combining acute (NFD).

vectors.name: reject-unsafe-integer
vectors.description: An integer beyond the 2^53-1 safe range is rejected (§4.3.2).

vectors.name: text-non-text-child-breaks-run
vectors.description: A non-text inline child breaks a run of text nodes: the surrounding text nodes are not merged across it (§4.3.1 item 4).

vectors.name: crdt-stripped-from-text-then-merges
vectors.description: crdt is stripped from a text node; the node, now bearing no distinguishing field, merges with its equal-marked neighbour (§4.3.1 items 1 and 4).

vectors.name: crdt-stripped-from-extension-block
vectors.description: crdt stripping is namespace-agnostic: it applies to an unknown namespace:type extension block just as to a core block (§4.3.1 item 1).

vectors.name: alpha-rename-nested-block-document-order
vectors.description: Block ids are assigned b0,b1,… in document order, and a nested block follows its parent (§4.3.1 item 5): blockquote b0 > paragraph b1, then paragraph b2.

vectors.name: alpha-rename-href-offset-suffix-and-unresolved
vectors.description: A #blockId reference is rewritten to the canonical name and keeps its /offset suffix (#sec/10-25 → #b0/10-25); an unresolved #ref (#ghost) is left verbatim; the marks re-sort after rewriting (§4.3.1 items 3 and 5).

vectors.name: reject-duplicate-block-id
vectors.description: Two blocks sharing an id in the identifier namespace is a duplicate-id error (§4.3.1 item 5).

vectors.name: text-id-boundary-preserved
vectors.description: A text node carrying an id is a merge boundary AND its id is preserved verbatim (anchor1 stays anchor1): a text-node id is not in the relabeled namespace (§4.3.1 items 4-5) and is content-significant (item 4 preserves it unchanged).

vectors.name: sha512-document-algorithm-agility
vectors.description: The document-ID algorithm follows the id prefix (sha512), never a hardcoded default (§3.3) — the same content under a different algorithm yields a different, correctly-prefixed id.

vectors.name: sha3-256-document-algorithm-agility
vectors.description: The document-ID algorithm follows the id prefix (sha3-256), never a hardcoded default (§3.3) — the same content under a different algorithm yields a different, correctly-prefixed id.

vectors.name: sha3-512-document-algorithm-agility
vectors.description: The document-ID algorithm follows the id prefix (sha3-512), never a hardcoded default (§3.3) — the same content under a different algorithm yields a different, correctly-prefixed id.

vectors.name: reject-duplicate-key-nested-object
vectors.description: A duplicate key in a NESTED object (not just top-level) is rejected by strict parsing (§4.3.2).

vectors.name: reject-duplicate-key-in-array-element
vectors.description: A duplicate key inside an array element is rejected (§4.3.2).

vectors.name: reject-escape-equivalent-duplicate-key
vectors.description: Escape-equivalent keys collide: "a" and "\u0061" are the same key, so this is a duplicate (§4.3.2).

vectors.name: reject-unknown-hash-algorithm
vectors.description: An unknown / non-registered hash algorithm (md5) is rejected; a verifier follows the value prefix and never a hardcoded default, but an unsupported algorithm is refused (§3.3).

vectors.name: reject-unregistered-asset-reference
vectors.description: An assets/ reference that matches no registered asset is a canonicalization error (§4.3.1 item 2).

vectors.name: reject-asset-category-without-index
vectors.description: A manifest.assets category with no supplied index cannot resolve its references (§4.3.1 item 2).

vectors.name: reject-malformed-registered-hash
vectors.description: A registered asset hash that is not a well-formed algorithm:hexdigest ("sha256:XYZ") is rejected (§4.3.1 item 2).

vectors.name: reject-unpaired-surrogate
vectors.description: A stored string containing an unpaired surrogate (lone high surrogate U+D83D) is not well-formed Unicode and is rejected (§4.3.2).

vectors.name: reject-conflicting-hashes-for-one-path
vectors.description: Two assets resolving to one archive path with conflicting hashes make resolution order-dependent and are rejected (§4.3.1 item 2).

vectors.name: reject-duplicate-key-dublin-core
vectors.description: A duplicate key in the Dublin Core part is rejected end-to-end (§4.3.2).

vectors.name: reject-duplicate-key-asset-index
vectors.description: A duplicate key in an asset index is rejected end-to-end (§4.3.2).

vectors.name: reject-sub-block-id-collides-with-block-id
vectors.description: A sub-block id (equation line) colliding with a block id in the shared namespace is a duplicate-id error (§4.3.1 item 5).

vectors.name: reject-unresolved-generated-name-reference
vectors.description: A #ref that spells a generated canonical name (#b0) but resolves to no such block is rejected — left verbatim it would be indistinguishable from a real reference, collapsing two documents (§4.3.1 item 5).

vectors.name: reject-block-level-nfc-split-across-nodes
vectors.description: Text nodes each individually NFC but whose concatenated run is NFD (a combining mark split from its base across an unmergeable boundary) is rejected at the block level (§4.3.2).

vectors.name: reject-malformed-dublin-core-term
vectors.description: A projected Dublin Core term with a malformed value (title as a number) — SHOULD-level: §4.3 does not explicitly mandate rejecting it (the type is defined by Metadata §7.2, not the §4.3 projection), so an implementation that passes it through is not failed.

vectors.name: reject-non-string-array-element-in-term
vectors.description: A non-string element in a projected Dublin Core array term (creator ["A",42]) — SHOULD-level for the same reason (Metadata §7.2, not §4.3).

vectors.name: metadata-five-terms-with-description
vectors.description: All five projected terms kept incl. a non-empty description; title and description stay scalar, creator/subject/language become arrays (§4.3.1).

vectors.name: metadata-array-element-order-preserved
vectors.description: A projected array term keeps its authored element order verbatim (§4.3.1).

vectors.name: metadata-projects-to-empty
vectors.description: When no projected term survives, metadata is an empty object (§4.3.1).

vectors.name: asset-svg-src-resolves
vectors.description: An svg src that is an archive-relative asset path resolves to the asset content hash (§4.3.1 item 2).

vectors.name: asset-svg-inline-content-untouched
vectors.description: An svg with inline content and no src has nothing to resolve and is left verbatim (§4.3.1 item 2).

vectors.name: asset-signature-image-resolves-ref-verbatim
vectors.description: A signature block’s image resolves to a content hash; its digitalSignatureRef (not an asset path) is left verbatim (§4.3.1 item 2).

vectors.name: asset-link-href-resolves
vectors.description: A link mark href that is an archive-relative asset path resolves to the asset content hash (§4.3.1 item 2).

vectors.name: asset-carveout-url-scheme-verbatim
vectors.description: An image src carrying a URL scheme (https://) is not an asset path and is left verbatim (§4.3.1 item 2).

vectors.name: asset-carveout-external-verbatim
vectors.description: An image src marked external:true is left verbatim even though it looks like an assets/ path (§4.3.1 item 2).

vectors.name: asset-non-assets-relative-href-verbatim
vectors.description: A relative href that is not under assets/ (chapter2.html) is left verbatim (§4.3.1 item 2).

vectors.name: asset-link-hrefs-resolve-before-dedup
vectors.description: Two links to different paths that share one content hash collapse to a single mark: resolution happens before mark dedup (§4.3.1 items 2-3).

vectors.name: asset-sha512-hash-spliced-into-sha256-document
vectors.description: A sha512 asset hash is spliced verbatim into a document whose own id is sha256 — the asset hash is opaque data, not the document algorithm (§4.3.1 item 2; §3.3).

vectors.name: asset-src-dot-segments-normalized
vectors.description: An asset src with ./ or ../ segments is path-normalized before resolving (§4.3.1 item 2).

vectors.name: asset-resolution-recurses-into-extension-block
vectors.description: Asset resolution is namespace-agnostic: a link inside an unknown namespace:type extension block resolves like any other (§4.3.1 item 2).

vectors.name: asset-aliasof-entry-with-own-path-resolves
vectors.description: An aliasOf entry that carries its own path and hash still resolves by its path (§4.3.1 item 2).

vectors.name: numbers-safe-integer-and-float-pass
vectors.description: A safe integer (2^53-1) and an ordinary float pass through unchanged; only the derived display is stripped (§4.3.1-2).

vectors.name: relabel-anchor-mark-id-and-ref
vectors.description: An anchor mark id joins the relabeled namespace and a #ref to it is rewritten consistently (§4.3.1 item 5).

vectors.name: relabel-marks-resort-after-rewrite
vectors.description: Rewriting hrefs can change mark sort order, so marks are re-sorted after rewriting: input [#k,#m] rewrites to [#b1,#b0] then re-sorts to [#b0,#b1] (§4.3.1 items 3, 5).

vectors.name: relabel-dangling-hash-b-no-digits-verbatim
vectors.description: A #ref of "#b" (b with NO digits) does not spell a generated canonical name, so it is left verbatim (§4.3.1 item 5).

vectors.name: relabel-academic-mark-refs
vectors.description: Academic theorem-ref/equation-ref/algorithm-ref mark targets are rewritten to canonical names; the algorithm-ref line label is a separate namespace, left verbatim (§4.3.1 item 5).

vectors.name: relabel-semantic-ref-and-presentation-reference
vectors.description: semantic:ref and presentation:reference block targets are rewritten to canonical names (§4.3.1 item 5).

vectors.name: relabel-footnote-block-id-not-mark-id
vectors.description: A footnote BLOCK id is relabeled, but the footnote MARK id (a separate namespace) is left verbatim, so no false duplicate arises (§4.3.1 item 5).

vectors.name: relabel-separate-namespace-mark-fields-verbatim
vectors.description: glossary.ref and citation.refs address separate namespaces and are left verbatim even when equal to a block id (§4.3.1 item 5).

vectors.name: relabel-subfigure-and-equation-line-ids
vectors.description: Subfigure ids and equation-line ids join the relabeled namespace (§4.3.1 item 5).

vectors.name: relabel-signature-signer-id-verbatim
vectors.description: A signature signer.id is a Person identifier (a named sub-object), not a content anchor, so it is left verbatim and does not collide with a block id (§4.3.1 item 5).

vectors.name: relabel-bibliography-entry-key-collides-with-block-id
vectors.description: A bibliography entry key is left verbatim even when it equals a block id being relabeled — they are separate namespaces (§4.3.1 item 5).

vectors.name: nfc-preserving-split-across-nodes
vectors.description: Text nodes each individually NFC, whose concatenation is also NFC (“caf” + precomposed é), still canonicalize; differing marks prevent merge (§4.3.2).

vectors.name: marks-sort-utf16-astral-before-ffff
vectors.description: Marks sort by UTF-16 code-unit order: an astral href (U+10000, high surrogate 0xD800) sorts BEFORE U+FFFF (§4.3.1 item 3). Both hrefs are non-asset relative refs, left verbatim.

vectors.name: purity-subblock-label-eq-exp
vectors.description: Sub-block label purity: differing subfigure/equation-line labels (sub-a/eq-exp) canonicalize to the same document id (§4.3.1 item 5).

vectors.name: purity-subblock-label-eq-99
vectors.description: Sub-block label purity: differing subfigure/equation-line labels (sub-z/eq-99) canonicalize to the same document id (§4.3.1 item 5).

vectors.name: bibliography-entry-key-significant-a
vectors.description: A bibliography entry key is content-significant: changing it (smith2024) while a citation ref stays fixed changes the document id, closing a collision (§4.3.1 item 5).

vectors.name: bibliography-entry-key-significant-b
vectors.description: A bibliography entry key is content-significant: changing it (smith2099) while a citation ref stays fixed changes the document id, closing a collision (§4.3.1 item 5).

vectors.name: reference-structure-significant-a
vectors.description: Relabeling must not over-collapse: two documents whose only difference is which block a link targets (#h1) get different ids (§4.3.1 item 5).

vectors.name: reference-structure-significant-b
vectors.description: Relabeling must not over-collapse: two documents whose only difference is which block a link targets (#h2) get different ids (§4.3.1 item 5).
